description: Use early in ideation or when stuck generating ideas.

Orchestrate multi-agent discussions when you need multiple perspectives or want agents to collaborate. Can use shared BMAD planning capability rounds: capability:zoom-out, capability:tdd, capability:ubiquitous-language, and capability:grill-me.

Run a generic local Codex/BMAD loop with explicit goal input, capability:zoom-out, capability:tdd, capability:ubiquitous-language, capability:grill-me, fresh non-main branch safety, quality gates, install/refresh evidence, and checkpointed continuation.

Run BMAD repository improvement as a predefined bmad-loop instance. Requires explicit operator goal, goal_ref, or scope; consumes capability:zoom-out, capability:tdd, capability:ubiquitous-language, and capability:grill-me; preserves SI-AUTO aliases and checkpointed continuation.
Orient users and recommend next BMAD steps, including shared BMAD planning capabilities when intent fits: capability:zoom-out, capability:tdd, capability:ubiquitous-language, and capability:grill-me.

Operate BMAD Workspace Sessions from Codex, including Repo Intake, BMAD Work Packets, Review Manifest, Evidence Index, archive diff, manual results, Manual Closeout, Worktree Review, archives, destroy, grant-governed base improvement, and shared planning setup refs for capability:zoom-out, capability:tdd, capability:ubiquitous-language, and capability:grill-me.
Use when LLM needs to understand available docs without loading everything.
Use when doc becomes too large (>500 lines) to manage effectively.
Use after drafting to polish written content.
output-location: three-column markdown table with suggested fixes

description: Use when doc produced from multiple subprocesses or needs structural improvement.
Use for quality assurance or before finalizing deliverables. Code Review in other modules runs this automatically, but also useful for document reviews.
Use alongside adversarial review for orthogonal coverage — method-driven not attitude-driven.
Use when you need token-efficient distillates that preserve all information for downstream LLM consumption.
required: adjacent to source document or specified output_path

description: Use when you want to change how an agent or workflow behaves — add persistent facts, swap templates, insert activation hooks, or customize menus. Scans what's customizable, picks the right scope (agent vs workflow), writes the override to _bmad/custom/, and verifies the merge. No TOML hand-authoring required.
